meta.name: French Country Beef Stew
meta.subtitle: Rustic red-wine–style braise with root vegetables (Bourguignon-adjacent).

source.name: ssa.cuisines.french_country
notes: Canonical SSA recipe: deterministic steps, adapter-friendly timers/checks, and stable ids.
Designed for Dutch oven on stovetop + oven; includes stovetop-only adaptation notes.

summary: A classic French-country beef stew: deeply browned beef, aromatic mirepoix, wine-and-stock braise, and tender vegetables. Make ahead friendly and even better the next day.

yield.notes: Serves 6 generously, or 8 with bread/noodles and a salad.

notes: Plan for a longer braise; active time is mainly browning and prep.
Flavor improves after resting overnight.
Deep savory beef flavor with gentle wine acidity and herb aroma.
Optional mushroom add-in boosts umami.

optional.label: Oven
optional.notes: Oven braise is steadier and less likely to scorch; recommended if available.

notes: Gluten present only if flour is used for thickening. Use cornstarch slurry or reduce instead.

ingredients.label: Beef chuck roast, cut into 1.5-inch cubes
notes: Other good cuts: boneless short rib, brisket point.
Trim large hard fat; keep some marbling for flavor.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: Start here and adjust at the end—stock and reduction concentrate salt.

ingredients.label: All-purpose flour
notes: Optional. For gluten-free: omit and thicken by reduction or cornstarch slurry.

ingredients.label: Unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: Optional for finishing or sautéing mushrooms/pearl onions.

ingredients.label: Tomato paste
notes: Browning the paste deepens flavor and color.

ingredients.label: Dry red wine
notes: Choose a dry wine you’d drink. Substitute: more stock + 1–2 tbsp balsamic (optional).

ingredients.label: Fresh rosemary
ingredients.notes: Optional but great with beef; remove before serving.

ingredients.label: Cremini mushrooms, quartered
notes: Optional but classic. Add later so they keep texture.

ingredients.label: Yukon gold potatoes, cut into 1.5-inch chunks
notes: Optional if serving with mashed potatoes/noodles/bread instead.

ingredients.label: Red wine vinegar
ingredients.notes: Optional finishing brightness if stew tastes flat.

steps.title: Prep beef and aromatics
instructions: Pat beef very dry (browning requires dry surface).
Season beef with salt and pepper.
Dice onion, cut carrots/celery, mince garlic, and portion herbs.
checks: Vegetables are similar size for even cooking.

steps.title: Optional: light flour dredge for thicker sauce
instructions: If using flour: sprinkle over beef and toss to lightly coat.
Shake off excess—too much flour can make sauce pasty.
checks: Beef has a light dusting, not a heavy coating.

steps.title: Brown the beef deeply (build flavor)
instructions: Heat a Dutch oven (or heavy pot) over medium-high heat. Add olive oil.
Brown beef in batches (do not crowd). Sear 2–3 minutes per side until deep brown.
Transfer browned beef to a bowl and repeat with remaining beef.
checks: Browning is dark golden to brown (not gray).
Fond (browned bits) is forming on the pot bottom.

steps.title: Cook vegetables and brown tomato paste
instructions: Lower heat to medium. Add onion (and celery if using) with a pinch of salt; cook 3–4 minutes.
Add carrots and cook 3 minutes more.
Add garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in tomato paste and cook 1–2 minutes, scraping and stirring, until it darkens slightly.

steps.title: Deglaze with wine (or stock) and dissolve the fond
instructions: Pour in red wine (if using). Bring to a simmer while scraping up browned bits from the bottom.
Simmer 2–3 minutes to reduce slightly and cook off harsh alcohol edge.
If not using wine: deglaze with 1 cup stock instead.
checks: Bottom of pot is mostly clean (fond dissolved into liquid).

steps.title: Add stock, herbs, and beef; bring to a gentle simmer
instructions: Add stock, bay leaves, thyme, and optional rosemary.
Return beef (and any collected juices) to the pot.
Bring to a gentle simmer (small bubbles), not a hard boil.
checks: Liquid level comes close to covering beef; add a splash more stock if needed.

steps.title: Braise until beef is fork-tender
instructions: Cover and braise until beef is tender: 1.5–2 hours.
Preferred method: oven braise at 325°F (163°C) for steady heat.
Stovetop method: keep on the lowest simmer and stir occasionally to prevent scorching.

temperature.notes: If oven braising. Stovetop: maintain a gentle simmer.

checks: Beef yields easily to a fork (not chewy).
Sauce is developing body and color.

steps.title: Add potatoes/mushrooms/pearl onions (optional) and finish simmer
instructions: If using potatoes: add them now (so they don’t disintegrate).
If using mushrooms and/or pearl onions: add now for the final 20–30 minutes.
Simmer uncovered or partially covered until vegetables are tender.
checks: Potatoes are tender but not falling apart.
Mushrooms are cooked through and still have shape.

steps.title: Adjust thickness and seasoning
instructions: Remove bay leaves and herb stems.
Taste and adjust salt and pepper.
If sauce is thin: simmer uncovered 5–10 minutes to reduce.
Gluten-free thickening option: stir 1 tbsp cornstarch with 2 tbsp cold water, then drizzle in while simmering until slightly thickened.
Optional brightness: add 1 tsp red wine vinegar (or a squeeze of lemon) if flavors feel flat.
checks: Seasoning tastes balanced (savory, not overly salty).

steps.title: Rest briefly, then serve
instructions: Let stew rest 10 minutes off heat (flavor settles).
Serve with chopped parsley and a side: crusty bread, noodles, mashed potatoes, or rice.
checks: Stew has cooled slightly and thickened a bit.

notes: A sharp green salad balances the richness.
If serving with bread, use the stew as the centerpiece meal.
Cool quickly and refrigerate. Flavor improves overnight.
Skim solidified fat next day for a cleaner sauce (optional).
Freeze in meal-size portions. Thaw overnight in fridge.
Potatoes can change texture after freezing; if freezer-focused, omit potatoes and serve with fresh starch later.
Reheat gently at a simmer. Add a splash of stock/water if too thick.
Avoid hard boiling to keep beef tender.

core: Brown in batches to avoid steaming and undercooked surfaces.
Maintain a gentle simmer during braise to prevent toughening and scorching.
Cool leftovers promptly and reheat to steaming hot.

notes: If you don’t want wine: use all stock and optionally add 1–2 tsp vinegar at the end for balance.
If stew tastes bitter: reduce less aggressively and add a small pinch of sugar or extra carrots (natural sweetness).
For deeper flavor: make a day ahead and reheat; skim fat and adjust seasoning just before serving.
Replace wine with additional stock. Finish with 1–2 tsp red wine vinegar (or lemon) for brightness.
Skip flour. Thicken by reducing uncovered, or use a cornstarch slurry at the end.
After deglazing and returning beef, cover and braise at 325°F for 90–120 minutes.
Less stirring needed; lower risk of scorching.

remixIdeas: Beef stew pot pie (top with biscuits or puff pastry)
Stew as a filling for savory hand pies
